auto_merge.commit_message:
Problem:
`foldcolumn` is empty for virtual lines above the start of a nested
fold.

Solution:
For virtual lines, compute the outer fold level and display it by
reusing the logic from `fill_foldcolumn`.
auto_merge.commit_title: fix(folds): foldcolumn is interrupted for virtual line above nested fold (#39999)

repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

## Problem
#39998 

## Solution
For virtual lines, compute the outer fold level and display it by reusing the logic from `fill_foldcolumn`.

I wanted to avoid adding branches to the condition chain as I think it would have made it harder to maintain. This way the code needed to fix the issue is separate from the old code.

I also extracted the last three `else if` branches into an `inline` function to not duplicate logic. If that's unwanted (due to it being old vim code) let me know.

Closes #39998

comment_data.body: I think this branch should also be moved into `foldcolumn_overflow_char()`. Then it should no longer be named `foldcolumn_overflow_char()`, though.
Changed it to `foldcolumn_sep_char` and updated docstring

I think this should be
```suggestion
        fill_foldcolumn(wp, stcp->foldinfo, lnum, 0, fdc, get_vim_var_nr(VV_VIRTNUM) < 0,
                        NULL, stcp->fold_vcol, fold_buf);
```

This test is mostly a duplicate of the test in fold_spec.lua. I think instead of duplicating the test, it's better to add some `set statuscolumn=%C` lines in fold_spec.lua, and then use `screen:expect_unchanged()` to check for the same state, like what is done in sign_spec.lua lines 182-183.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -884,6 +884,60 @@ describe('statuscolumn', function()
     ]])
   end)
 
+  it('foldcolumn for virtual lines shows correctly', function()
+    api.nvim_set_option_value('statuscolumn', '%C', {})
+    api.nvim_buf_set_lines(0, 0, -1, true, { 'line 1', 'line 2', 'line 3', 'line 4' })
+
+    local ns = api.nvim_create_namespace('ns')
+    api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(0, ns, 1, 0, { virt_lines = { { { 'below line 2', '' } } } })
+    api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(
+      0,
+      ns,
+      2,
+      0,
+      { virt_lines_above = true, virt_lines = { { { 'above line 3', '' } } } }
+    )
+
+    feed('gg0')
+    command('1,4fold | 1,4foldopen')
+    command('3,4fold | 3,4foldopen ')
+    command('set foldcolumn=1')
+    screen:expect([[
+          {7:-}{8: }^line 1                                             |
+          {7:│}{8: }line 2                                             |
+          {7:│}{8: }below line 2                                       |
+          {7:│}{8: }above line 3                                       |
+          {7:-}{8: }line 3                                             |
+          {7:2}{8: }line 4                                             |
+          {1:~                                                    }|*7
+                                                               |
+        ]])
+
+    command('set foldcolumn=2')
+    screen:expect([[
+          {7:- }{8: }^line 1                                            |
+          {7:│ }{8: }line 2                                            |
+          {7:│ }{8: }below line 2                                      |
+          {7:│ }{8: }above line 3                                      |
+          {7:│-}{8: }line 3                                            |
+          {7:││}{8: }line 4                                            |
+          {1:~                                                    }|*7
+                                                               |
+        ]])
+
+    command('2,4fold | 2,4foldopen')
+    screen:expect([[
+          {7:- }{8: }^line 1                                            |
+          {7:│-}{8: }line 2                                            |
+          {7:││}{8: }below line 2                                      |
+          {7:││}{8: }above line 3                                      |
+          {7:2-}{8: }line 3                                            |
+          {7:23}{8: }line 4                                            |
+          {1:~                                                    }|*7
+                                                               |
+        ]])
+  end)
+

comment_regular_data.body: Fixed another edge case for `foldcolumn > 1` and added a test for it

Test failure with ASAN. This possibly means that the code is using uninitialized memory.

There is also another problem here: when drawing 'statuscolumn' for virtual lines it's not possible to tell if the current virtual line is above or below `v:lnum`. This matters here as for virtual lines, fold column in 'statuscolumn' needs to use fold symbol from the line above, which is line `v:lnum - 1` for an "above" virtual line, but line `v:lnum` for a "below" virtual line.

- Fixed ASAN by also checking `foldinfo.fi_level` (other `foldinfo` values are not always initialized).
- Fixed status column case by passing `lnum` through `statuscol_T`, which might also be useful for #40187. Also added a test case to `statuscolumn_spec`.

> Fixed status column case by passing lnum through statuscol_T, which might also be useful for

If it is, I would be surprised, at least regarding what the behavior is for 'foldcolumn'. Shouldn't that logic use the same line number that 'statuscolumn' uses (which accounts for the virt_line below/above discrepancy), rather than `winlinevars_T.lnum`?
